account.usage_instructions: Authenticated account operations. Use `account list` when a list-shaped result is needed for standard filtering or property selection, and `account get` when you want the single account object directly.

get.help: Get the authenticated Pinterest user account.
get.usage_instructions: Get the authenticated Pinterest account as a single object. Use `--properties/-p` to limit fields or `--table/-t` for a field/value view.

arguments.name: account_id
arguments.help: Authenticated Pinterest account ID to validate against the current account

options.name: --ad-account-id
options.help: Pinterest ad account ID for shared business access requests

list.help: List the authenticated Pinterest account as a single-item collection.
list.usage_instructions: Return the authenticated Pinterest account as a single-item collection so standard list flags work. Use `--properties/-p` to limit fields, `--filter/-f` for client-side filtering, and `--table/-t` for a compact summary.

options.name: --limit
options.help: RANGE [x>=1]  Maximum number of accounts to return

options.name: --ad-account-id
options.help: Pinterest ad account ID for shared business access requests

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication and profile management. Use these commands to log in with Pinterest OAuth, inspect saved credential state, refresh tokens, and manage named auth profiles.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Start Pinterest OAuth login and save credentials for the active or named profile. Use `--profile/-p` to target a profile and `--force/-F` to re-authenticate with fresh tokens.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Clear saved Pinterest credentials and session state for the selected profile. Use `--profile/-p` to target a non-default profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Nested authentication profile management under `auth profiles`. Use these commands to create, inspect, switch, and delete named Pinterest auth profiles.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a named Pinterest auth profile from `.env.example`. Pass the required profile name, then run `pinterest auth login --profile NAME` to populate credentials.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a saved Pinterest auth profile and its runtime data. Pass the required profile name and add `--force/-F` to skip confirmation.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get one saved auth profile by name. Pass the required profile name and use `--table/-t` for field/value output.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all saved Pinterest auth profiles. Use `--filter/-f`, `--limit/-l`, and `--properties/-p` like any other list command, or `--table/-t` for a concise profile table.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Refresh the saved Pinterest OAuth access token using the stored refresh token. Use `--profile/-p` to target a specific profile.

status.usage_instructions: Show saved Pinterest auth state across profiles in the standard auth-status JSON shape. Use `--table/-t` for a human-readable status table or `--profile/-p` to narrow to one profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run explicit Pinterest authentication verification checks. Use `--verbose/-v` for more detail, `--table/-t` for table output, and `--profile/-p` to scope the test.

boards.usage_instructions: Board reads for the authenticated Pinterest user. Use `boards list` to browse boards with filtering and field selection, then `boards get BOARD_ID` for full details on one board.

get.help: Get a specific Pinterest board by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get one Pinterest board by ID. Pass the required `BOARD_ID` positional argument and use `--table/-t` or `--properties/-p` when you want a narrower view.

options.name: --ad-account-id
options.help: Pinterest ad account ID for shared business access requests

list.help: List Pinterest boards for the authenticated user.
list.usage_instructions: List Pinterest boards for the authenticated user. Use `--filter/-f privacy:eq:SECRET` for documented privacy filtering, `--limit/-l` to control API page size, and `--properties/-p` to narrow output fields.

options.name: --limit
options.help: RANGE [x>=1]  Maximum number of boards to return

options.name: --ad-account-id
options.help: Pinterest ad account ID for shared business access requests

cache.usage_instructions: Response cache maintenance. Use `cache clear` when you need to remove cached Pinterest API results for the active profile.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Delete cached Pinterest API responses for the active profile. Use when you need the next command to fetch fresh API data instead of cached results.

pins.usage_instructions: Pin reads for the authenticated Pinterest user. Use `pins list` to browse recent pins with standard CLI list flags, then `pins get PIN_ID` for one pin's detail object.

get.help: Get a specific Pinterest pin by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get one Pinterest pin by ID. Pass the required `PIN_ID` positional argument and add `--table/-t` or `--properties/-p` to control the presentation.

options.name: --ad-account-id
options.help: Pinterest ad account ID for shared business access requests

list.help: List Pinterest pins for the authenticated user.
list.usage_instructions: List Pinterest pins for the authenticated user. Use `--limit/-l` to cap returned pins, `--filter/-f` for standard client-side filtering such as `board_owner.username:contains:value`, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

options.name: --limit
options.help: RANGE [x>=1]  Maximum number of pins to return

options.name: --ad-account-id
options.help: Pinterest ad account ID for shared business access requests

usage_instructions: Use this tool for Pinterest API v5 reads around the authenticated account, boards, and pins. Commands follow `pinterest <group> <action>` and list-style commands support `--table/-t`, `--filter/-f`, `--limit/-l`, and `--properties/-p`. Authenticate first with `pinterest auth login` before running API-backed commands.
